sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.93 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$N G Q$be an extension of nilpotent groups, with$Q$finitely generated, which splits at every prime. Does the extension split? This is known to be true if$N$is finite or commutative. { P.\,Hilton} No, not always (K.\,Lorensen, Math. Proc. Cambridge Philos. Soc., { 123}, no.\,2 (1998), 213--215).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
